evals.prompt: I've got the user-management epic loaded in ohno with 3 stories and about 9 tasks. I want to work through them in semi-auto mode. The project is a Next.js app at ~/Projects/acme/webapp. Let's get started.
evals.expected_output: Claude initializes ohno session, calls get_session_context and get_next_task, sets up worktree for first task, dispatches implementer agent, works through tasks with pauses at story boundaries.
expectations: Calls get_session_context() MCP tool to check for existing session state
Calls get_next_task() to find the first available task
References semi-auto mode behavior (pause at story boundaries, skip task boundaries)
Mentions worktree setup or isolation for the task
Dispatches an implementer subagent for the first task
Describes the checkpoint behavior for story completion

evals.prompt: I was working on the payments epic yesterday and got through story-1 and half of story-2. The session context in ohno should have details. Continue where I left off in auto mode.
evals.expected_output: Claude calls get_session_context to find previous state, identifies the in-progress task, reads handoff notes, resumes execution without redoing completed work. Uses auto mode (pause only at epic boundary).
expectations: Calls get_session_context() to retrieve previous session state
Looks for handoff notes or in-progress tasks from previous session
Does not re-execute already completed tasks
References auto mode behavior (skip task and story checkpoints, pause at epic boundary)
Resumes from the specific in-progress task

evals.prompt: Run through all remaining tasks in the notifications epic overnight. Unattended mode, parallel with 3 agents. I'll review in the morning.
evals.expected_output: Claude configures unattended mode (never pauses), sets up parallel execution with 3 agents, verifies pre-flight checks, starts autonomous task execution.
expectations: References unattended mode (no pauses at any boundary)
Configures parallel execution with multiple agents
Mentions or performs pre-flight checks before starting
Describes how parallel tasks avoid dependencies or conflicts
Plans for session summary or handoff notes at the end
